John wick 4 somehow managed to raise the bar on an already top class action series. 

What an incredible experience. The dialogue, the sets, the lighting, and ofcourse the action, were all peerlessly put together. 

There's nothing else like the John wick franchise. It started off humbly with the first one, and gracefully expanded into a series that is equal parts bold and restrained, but entirely fresh.

It's safe to say at this point that no one does action better than this team. JW4 is a masterpiece of not just action cinema, but cinema in general.

I was watching the Despicable Me movies yesterday.  They are absolutely hilarious.  It is funny how cartoons are absent of political correctness - and work because they let both the writers and the audience have a good time.  John Wick does the same.  A super hero movie doesn't have to be too serious -- it can be fun, outrageous, and over the top as long as the audience enjoys themselves. In the early years, Keanu was never really been taken seriously as an actor.  John Wick is a pile of gold for him because it has no rules -- and it is just seriously fun.

I know nothing about the lore or story beyond these movies. My impression has been for some time now that every aspect of these movies is intentional. There is a higher force at work in the world of john wick. I think the scenarios have been manipulated intentionally from the start by someone. Watch the expressions and reactions carefully and you will see what im talking about. Somone wanted him back on the board and i think winston is a part in that. The broke continental rules by giving him the red ring note in the first film. Winston supposedly betraying him in the end of 3 but showing no worry about it. He is pulling strings. There is also what i believe to be a supernatural quality to the world of john wick. The apparent ability to disappear and reappear in the different location, i call it slipping dimensions. There is also the seeming growth of john wicks strength and endurence as the story progresses. Almost like the more he gives himself to the task at hand the stronger he grows. Many many little details throughout these movies. I almost think these movies take place in a realm parallel to ours. The fights and shooting and never the slightest hint of law enforcement. Kinda like the people of the real world can see some of the effects of these fights but never the whole picture. Occasionally people run but take the train station of 3. Several people are killed and there is not a single reaction. When he first gets of the train and is attacked but that one dudes goons kills them, in the shot facing backwards as they walk forwards, there are no bodies or blood. I think these movies hint at larger things but never discuss them. Great movies.

I don't get the appeal for this series. I saw one of the movies on tv, there was literally 0 plot and almost no dialogue, even from the protagonist Keanu's character. It's just nonstop action from start to finish. John Wick is the last remaining vestige of the once great action movie genre, but it's a joke. There's no story, no characters, no babes, no badass one-liners. Even the action feels downright videogamey at times. Watching this film is like a 2 hour compliation of every Mortal Kombat Fatality, but I'd wager Netherrealm puts more creativity into the MK Fatalities than the directors of John Wick do with the action.

John Wick started as a mid-budget indie action film and now it's a multi-million dollar franchise filled with an all-star cast of A-list actors. But even with all these new sequels, the franchise (so far) still manages to stay just as faithful to its humble roots. Which is very impressive because usually the third film is where things start to deviate from what made the first or two films great in the first place. John Wick 4 seems like it'll deliver what the previous three film had given us the audience, so this is definitely one movie I'll be looking forward to seeing in 2023.

This is the final trailer for the upcoming action movie, John Wick: Chapter 4, set to be released in 2023. The trailer provides the audience with a first look at the new plot details in the sequel of this popular action franchise.

The trailer showcases that Chapter 4 will continue to pursue the high-quality action style, with bloody and violent action scenes. However, it promises to have new elements to appeal to the audience. The trailer also introduces new actors joining the cast, such as Donnie Yen and Hiroyuki Sanada, along with the return of many actors who appeared in previous installments of the franchise.

In summary, the final trailer of John Wick: Chapter 4 brings excitement to the audience for an intense and worthy sequel of this famous action franchise.

As good as this series is, the story, the action, the intricacies of it, I truly believe none of it would be possible without the unbelievable dedication of Keanu Reeves. He puts EVERYTHING on the table every time.

This was my late grandfathers favourite movie series of all time. He had dementia and he could watch any of the 3 over and over again without getting tired of them, which was very rare even though his short term memory was non existent. We were both looking forward to this movie, so it was disappointing to say the least when they delayed the movie for so long, considering it had finished filming in 2021. This was my late grandfathers favourite movie series of all time. He had dementia and he could watch any of the 3 over and over again without getting tired of them, which was very rare even though his short term memory was non existent. We were both looking forward to this movie, so it was disappointing to say the least when they delayed the movie for so long, considering it had finished filming in 2021. He won’t ever be able to watch this movie unfortunately, as he passed away in May last year. And now you have released the trailer on his birthday, of all days.. which brings a smile to my face. I’ll be watching this with his thoughts in mind, knowing how much he would have loved it.
